purpose: This repository contains utilities for resolving and mapping accounts for loans in a lending and selling system. It queries account information from multiple sources (accounting system, loan operations database) and updates the selling database with the proper account mappings needed for financial operations.
businessFeatures: Account resolution for loans (purchase, return, interest, fee accounts)
Custom purchase account mapping based on loan details

apis.purpose: Retrieves account information for a given loan from the COS Lending Accounting service

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Core loan entity containing loan details needed for account resolution

dataEntities.name: LoanAccount
dataEntities.description: Maps a loan to its associated accounts with specific purposes/objectives

dataEntities.name: ObjectiveAccount
dataEntities.description: Account with a specific purpose such as purchase, return, interest income, etc.

dataEntities.name: FeeSweep
dataEntities.description: Configuration for fee sweeping operations between accounts

dataEntities.name: CustomPurchaseAccountMapping
dataEntities.description: Rules for mapping specific loans to custom purchase accounts based on loan details

findings.title: Exposed Database Credentials
findings.description: Database credentials and connection strings are stored in appsettings.json in plaintext. These should be moved to a secure secret store or environment variables, especially since these appear to be real development/QA servers.

findings.title: Hardcoded OAuth Client Credentials
findings.description: OAuth client credentials are configured in appsettings files rather than being injected through a secure mechanism like Azure Key Vault or environment variables.

findings.title: Inefficient Batch Processing with Large Memory Usage
findings.description: The application loads large amounts of loan data into memory when processing batches. This can lead to memory issues with large datasets. Consider implementing a streaming approach or pagination when processing large datasets.
